name: Google Vertex AI
what_it_does: Google Cloud's unified AI platform for accessing Gemini and other foundation models, training and deploying custom models, running batch predictions, and orchestrating ML pipelines.
use_cases: Accessing Gemini models (Pro, Flash, Ultra) via API within a Google Cloud environment with enterprise compliance
Building ML pipelines that combine Gemini with custom-trained models using Vertex Pipelines
Running batch prediction jobs over large datasets against Gemini or custom endpoints
Multimodal agent workflows using Gemini's native support for text, images, audio, and video in a single call
not_for: Teams not on GCP — AWS Bedrock or Azure OpenAI will integrate better with existing infrastructure
Quick API access to Gemini — the Google AI Studio / Gemini API (ai.google.dev) is simpler for non-GCP users
Small-scale or hobby projects where GCP account setup and billing complexity outweighs the benefit
best_when: You are building enterprise AI on GCP, need Gemini with data residency and VPC Service Controls, or want to combine Google's foundation models with your own trained models in a unified platform.
avoid_when: You want the simplest path to Gemini — use the Gemini API (ai.google.dev) directly. Vertex AI adds significant configuration overhead that only pays off at enterprise scale.

auth.notes: Application Default Credentials (ADC) is the recommended approach — uses gcloud CLI, Workload Identity for GKE, or a service account key file. OAuth 2.0 scopes required: cloud-platform. Service account keys are supported but discouraged in favor of Workload Identity Federation. Auth is more complex than AWS IAM roles for most developers.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $300 Google Cloud free trial credit for new accounts (90-day expiry). Gemini 1.5 Flash: free tier via AI Studio (not Vertex). Vertex AI itself has no persistent free tier after trial.
pricing.paid_tiers: Gemini 1.5 Pro: $1.25–$2.50/M input tokens, $5–$10/M output tokens (context-dependent). Gemini 1.5 Flash: $0.075–$0.15/M input, $0.30/M output. Batch prediction: 50% discount vs. online. Custom model training: per compute hour.
pricing.notes: The $300 credit is useful for experimentation. Production pricing is competitive with AWS Bedrock for Gemini models. Committed use discounts available. Batch prediction at half the online price is a significant advantage for offline agent workloads.

performance.rate_limits: Default: 60 requests/minute for Gemini 1.5 Pro; 1000 requests/minute for Flash. Token-level limits also apply. Increase via quota requests in Google Cloud Console. Batch prediction bypasses online quotas.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Google API errors use a standard Status object with code and message, but Vertex-specific errors (quota exceeded, model unavailable) can have generic messages. The Python SDK wraps errors in google.api_core.exceptions, which are well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GenerateContent is not idempotent. Batch prediction jobs support deduplication via job names. Long-running operations (training, batch prediction) return an Operation object that can be polled.
known_agent_gotchas: Application Default Credentials setup requires running 'gcloud auth application-default login' locally — missing this step causes cryptic authentication errors that look like network issues
The Vertex AI SDK (vertexai Python package) and the Google AI SDK (google-generativeai) are different packages with different APIs — even though both access Gemini, they are not interchangeable
Quota limits for online prediction are per project per region and reset every minute — agents hitting limits need exponential backoff with jitter
Enabling the Vertex AI API in a Google Cloud project is required before any calls succeed — a step that is easy to miss in automation scripts
VPC Service Controls (for data residency) add significant configuration complexity and must be set up before the first API call — retroactive application is disruptive

security_score_components.security_notes: Workload Identity Federation eliminates service account keys for GKE and other GCP workloads. VPC Service Controls enable data perimeter enforcement. CMEK (customer-managed encryption keys) supported. All traffic can stay within Google's private network via Private Google Access.
